ELPAC Level 2
Lexile 1300–1400L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Sweetgrass and Twelve Newtons

STS-MS8-L2-13001400-MSSCI8-MFJ-IFN-01 • MS Science Grade 8 • Newton’s Third Law and Collisions
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
In the Creator's Game, a player's stick and the ball push on each other at the very same moment, and the ball never gets pushed without pushing back. Some Indigenous coaches ask players to sit still and breathe before they ever pick up a stick, so they can feel their own body pressing into the ground and the ground pressing back. That is the same law working in a quiet room and in a hard collision.
When the teacher's mallet pushes on the bowl, what is the bowl doing to the mallet?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Kanien doing?
Look at the picture. Say one complete sentence about what Kanien is doing. Use exact words, not general words like thing or stuff.
2
What is Wapun holding?
Look at the picture. Say one complete sentence about the object Wapun is holding. Use its exact name and its color or material.
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Say one complete sentence about what the teacher is doing. Use exact words for her hands and the object.
4
What place is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Say one complete sentence about this place. Name one exact object that belongs in a place like this.
5
Tell me about the journal wall.
Look at the picture. Say one complete sentence about the journal wall. Use exact words for two things on it.
6
What is Tala wearing?
Look at the picture. Say one complete sentence about what Tala is wearing. Use exact color words.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: NGSS MS-PS2-1 — Newton’s Third Law and Collisions. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
